name: Ring of the Picos Trail (3 Massifs)
name_original: Tielve, Sotres, Refugio de Urriellu, Torre Cerredo, Bulnes — circular en el Parque Nacional Picos de Europa
region: Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa
description: This difficult 24.0-mile jogging route in Picos de Europa National Park offers dramatic peaks and gorges for experienced mountain runners.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa navigate a dramatic landscape of towering limestone mountains, deep valleys, and lush forests. The region is characterized by its alpine karst topography, featuring spiky summits, deeply carved canyons, and verdant slopes. Runners can find routes that wind through forests, alongside wild rivers, and ascend to high mountain pastures, offering varied terrain and elevation. Best jogging routes around Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa The most popular jogging route is Ring of Picos (3 Massifs), a 21.7 miles (35.0 km) trail that takes 8 hours 43 minutes to complete. This challenging route traverses the park's three main massifs, offering extensive mountain running. Another top favourite among local runners is Lakes of Covadonga Circular Trail, a difficult 4.2 miles (6.7 km) path. This route offers serene views around the famous glacial lakes. Local runners also love the Mirador del Cable-Bulnes, Refugio Cabaña Verónica, Torre Blanca — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa, an 8.2 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Jogging around Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa is defined by limestone peaks, deep gorges, and glacial lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging alpine trails. The routes in Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa?
A: There are over 60 dedicated running routes in Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa, offering a wide range of experiences from moderate paths to challenging alpine trails. Nearly 1000 runners have used komoot to explore this varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate jogging routes in Picos de Europa?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are 8 moderate jogging routes available. These often wind through lush forests or alongside wild rivers, providing a less strenuous experience compared to the high mountain trails. The area around the &lt;strong&gt;Lakes of Covadonga&lt;/strong&gt;, for instance, offers more accessible paths.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see while running?
A: Many running routes offer breathtaking views and pass by notable natural features. You can experience the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;Cares Gorge&lt;/strong&gt;, often called the 'Divine Gorge', with its impressive cliffs and rock-hewn tunnels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, accessible via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Dé Cable Car&lt;/a&gt;, provides spectacular panoramas. The serene glacial &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1228919629/lakes-of-covadonga-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes of Covadonga&lt;/a&gt; (Lago Enol and Lago de la Ercina) are also famous sights with stunning mountain backdrops.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Picos de Europa National Park?
A: Yes, the park features several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1228919629/lakes-of-covadonga-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes of Covadonga Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers serene views around the famous glacial lakes. For a more demanding experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229204791/picu-urriellu-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picu Urriellu Circuit&lt;/a&gt; provides extensive mountain running around the iconic Naranjo de Bulnes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Picos de Europa?
A: The running routes in Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning and varied natural landscapes, from the dramatic gorges to the towering limestone peaks, and the extensive network of trails catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Picos de Europa?
A: Picos de Europa National Park has specific regulations regarding dogs to protect its diverse wildlife. While dogs are generally allowed on trails, they must be kept on a leash at all times. It's always advisable to check the latest park rules before your visit, especially for specific areas or protected zones.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging options in the park?
A: While many routes are challenging, the areas around the &lt;strong&gt;Lakes of Covadonga&lt;/strong&gt; offer more accessible and relatively easy paths that are suitable for families. These trails provide a chance to enjoy the serene mountain views and spot grazing animals without extreme difficulty.

Q: What is the best time of year for trail running in Picos de Europa?
A: The best time for trail running in Picos de Europa is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is more stable, and most high-altitude trails are free of snow. However, even in summer, conditions can change rapidly, so always be prepared for varied mountain weather.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Naranjo de Bulnes (Picu Urriellu)?
A: Yes, the Central Massif is home to the iconic Naranjo de Bulnes (Picu Urriellu), which provides a spectacular backdrop for many trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229204791/picu-urriellu-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picu Urriellu Circuit&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers extensive mountain running with views of this formidable peak.

Q: Where can I find parking for running routes in Picos de Europa?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Popular starting points like Poncebos, Sotres, and areas near the Lakes of Covadonga often have designated parking. However, during peak season, these can fill up quickly. It's recommended to arrive early or consider using public transport options where available, especially for popular areas like Covadonga.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through charming villages or offer refreshment stops?
A: Many running routes connect or pass through picturesque mountain villages such as Poncebos, Sotres, and Caín. These villages often provide opportunities for cultural immersion, rest, and refreshment. Mountain huts, like Refugio Vega de Urriellu, also serve as destinations or waypoints for longer, more strenuous runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails?
A: The Picos de Europa offers highly diverse terrain. You can expect everything from challenging rocky, steep, and technical trails in the high mountains to more mellow 4x4 roads and accessible paths through lush forests and alongside wild rivers. The alpine karst topography features spiky summits, deeply carved canyons, and verdant slopes.
tips: A hidden gem in the mountains. It retains a traditional charm with its cobbled streets, stone houses and a historic church.
A picturesque village that is a good starting point for tours and where you can stop for a bite to eat afterwards.
The last section, about 200 meters long, is a climb and although the use of climbing equipment is not necessary, it is a technical section and not suitable for people without experience.

faqs.question: Is the Ring of the Picos Trail (3 Massifs) suitable for jogging or beginners?
faqs.answer: Despite some references to jogging, this trail is not suitable for casual jogging or beginners. It is an extremely challenging and strenuous multi-day trek designed for experienced mountaineers and mountain runners. The terrain is demanding, with significant elevation changes, rocky sections, and technical passages. The Komoot route provided is a segment of the larger Ring of the Picos, which typically covers 110-120 km with over 8,500 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is exceptionally varied and demanding. Expect a mix of lush valleys, forests, rocky passes, and alpine meadows. Much of the trail involves rocky ground, scree fields, and sharp limestone. Higher altitudes feature more technical and rocky paths, with sections requiring mild rock climbing without aid. Slippery, steep descents are common, especially in bad weather. Good hiking boots with ankle support and excellent grip are essential.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and scenic points along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking and diverse scenery. You'll experience dramatic limestone peaks, deep gorges like the iconic Cares Gorge, and stunning green valleys. The trail passes through charming mountain villages such as Tielve Village and Sotres Village. You'll also get views of famous peaks, including Torre Cerredo, the highest peak in the Picos de Europa.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to undertake this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this trail is typically from late spring to early autumn (June to September). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and mountain huts are open. However, even in summer, be prepared for rapidly changing weather conditions, including fog, rain, and strong winds, especially at higher altitudes. Avoid winter and early spring due to snow, ice, and extreme conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Ring of the Picos Trail (3 Massifs)?
faqs.answer: Dogs are generally allowed in the Picos de Europa National Park, but specific rules apply, especially regarding protected areas and wildlife. It's crucial to keep dogs on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife, particularly chamois and ground-nesting birds. Be aware that some mountain huts may not permit dogs inside, so plan your accommodation accordingly.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: There is no general entrance fee or permit required to access the Picos de Europa National Park or to hike this trail. However, certain activities or specific areas might have regulations. Always respect park rules, especially regarding waste, wildlife, and camping. If you plan to stay in mountain huts, it's highly recommended to book them in advance, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: Where does the Komoot route for the Ring of the Picos Trail (3 Massifs) start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route provided is a circular trail starting and ending near Tielve Village, passing through Sotres Village. Parking is generally available in these mountain villages, but spaces can be limited, especially during peak season. It's advisable to arrive early or consider public transport options if available to avoid parking difficulties.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths in the Picos de Europa?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers significant sections of other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the "El Anillo de Picos (3 Macizos)" and "Poncebos - Bulnes - Sotres - Tielve" routes. It also briefly touches upon segments of "El Anillo de Picos (Anillo Extrem)", "Jou de los Cabrones", and "Vega de Urriellu", among others, offering a comprehensive experience of the region's trail network.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Picos de Europa National Park is rich in biodiversity. Along the trail, you have a good chance of spotting chamois (a type of goat-antelope) in the rocky areas. Birdwatchers might be rewarded with sightings of various birds of prey, including griffon vultures and possibly even the rare bearded vulture. Keep an eye out for diverse flora, especially in the alpine meadows and valleys.

faqs.question: Are there mountain huts or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Picos de Europa region is well-equipped with mountain refuges (huts) that provide accommodation, food, and water. The full Ring of the Picos Trail is designed to connect many of these huts, offering a unique way to experience the mountains. For the Komoot route provided, you will pass near areas with refuges, such as Refugio de Urriellu. It is highly recommended to book your stays in advance, especially during the high season.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging trek?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied conditions, pack for a multi-day mountain trek. Essential items include sturdy, waterproof hiking boots, layered clothing suitable for all weather conditions (including waterproof and windproof outer layers), a backpack with sufficient capacity, navigation tools (map, compass, GPS device or app like komoot), plenty of water and high-energy snacks, a first-aid kit, headlamp, and sun protection. Trekking poles are highly recommended for stability on steep and rocky terrain.
